topic: A musician hears their song being performed by someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound drifted through the crowded bar, a clumsy, off-key rendition of "Echoes in the Void." My fingers, still tingling from the last chord I’d played, curled into fists on the worn mahogany bar. I watched the performer—a young man with too much enthusiasm and zero finesse—stumble through the melody. Every missed note felt like a personal slight, a deliberate desecration of the music I had poured my soul into. I leaned back, letting a slow, deliberate exhale escape my lips, the air tasting suddenly stale. He was just noise, a poorly executed imitation, and I felt a distinct tightening in my chest, a cold, hard judgment settling over me.

[story 2]

I stood near the edge of the stage, nursing a lukewarm drink, my gaze fixed on the figure on the guitar. When the familiar opening riff hit, it was warped, stretched thin, utterly devoid of the intricate layering I had painstakingly constructed. A sharp, unpleasant clench formed in my jaw. I shifted my weight, the movement stiff and economical. *Amateur.* The thought was immediate, sharp, and utterly dismissive. I watched as the musician visibly struggled to keep up, his brow furrowed with strain. It was tiresome, this display of inadequate skill. I took a slow sip of my drink, the ice clinking loudly, a small, irritating sound in the sudden silence of my internal assessment.

[story 3]

The music spilled out from the small venue, and it was *my* song. But the version being played was a cheap knock-off, clumsy and saccharine. I felt a distinct hardening around my eyes. I crossed my arms tightly over my chest, a barrier erected against the sound. Every clumsy strum, every misplaced vocal inflection, struck me as an insult to the artistry involved. I watched the performer sweat under the lights, his efforts transparently insufficient. A slow, almost imperceptible sneer touched the corner of my mouth. This was beneath me; this was sloppy work, and I found myself wishing the entire performance would simply dissolve into the floorboards.
